title: nepeta juncea subsp. xylorhiza
intro: Nepeta juncea subsp. xylorhiza, also known as Mountain Melaleuca, is a subspecies of Nepeta, a plant in the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Nepeta juncea subsp. xylorhiza can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
